service_line_config.name: gcp
service_line_config.tech_context: This project deploys on Google Cloud Platform. Key considerations: - Compute: Cloud Run, GKE, Cloud Functions, Compute Engine, App Engine - Database: Cloud SQL, Cloud Spanner, Firestore, Bigtable, Memorystore (Redis) - Storage: Cloud Storage, Filestore, Persistent Disks - Messaging: Pub/Sub, Cloud Tasks, Eventarc - Networking: VPC, Cloud Load Balancing, Cloud CDN, Cloud Armor, Private Service Connect - Security: IAM, Service Accounts, Secret Manager, VPC Service Controls, Cloud KMS - Monitoring: Cloud Monitoring, Cloud Logging, Cloud Trace, Error Reporting - DevOps: Cloud Build, Artifact Registry, Terraform, Deployment Manager - Data/AI: BigQuery, Vertex AI, Dataflow, Dataproc

artifact_overrides.module_extraction: Map modules to GCP project structure or application tiers. Consider separation by compute, data, integration, and security boundaries.
artifact_overrides.functional_req: - Reference specific GCP services for implementation approach - For data requirements, specify Cloud SQL vs Firestore vs Spanner vs BigQuery - For event-driven requirements, specify Pub/Sub topics and subscriptions - For serverless requirements, specify Cloud Run vs Cloud Functions tradeoffs
artifact_overrides.nonfunctional_req: - GCP service SLAs and composite availability targets - Auto-scaling configuration: Cloud Run concurrency, GKE HPA, min/max instances - Multi-region strategy and data residency requirements - Backup: Cloud SQL automated backups, Cloud Storage lifecycle policies - Cost: committed use discounts, billing alerts, resource labeling - Security: IAM least-privilege, VPC Service Controls, encryption (CMEK vs Google-managed) - Observability: Cloud Monitoring SLOs, uptime checks, alert policies
artifact_overrides.task: - Infrastructure: Terraform modules, GCP project setup, API enablement - Networking: VPC configuration, subnets, firewall rules, load balancers - Compute: Cloud Run/GKE/Functions deployment, container configuration - Data: database provisioning, schema management, migration scripts - Security: IAM roles, service accounts, Secret Manager, VPC Service Controls - Messaging: Pub/Sub topics, subscriptions, dead-letter configuration - Monitoring: dashboards, SLOs, alert policies, log-based metrics - DevOps: Cloud Build triggers, Artifact Registry, deployment pipelines
artifact_overrides.test_case: - Test auto-scaling under load (Cloud Run concurrency, GKE pod scaling) - Test Pub/Sub message delivery with acknowledgment and retry scenarios - Test service account permissions with least-privilege verification - Test Cloud SQL failover and backup restoration - Test VPC firewall rules and Private Service Connect connectivity - Verify IAM policy compliance across all projects
artifact_overrides.architecture: - GCP organization, folder, and project hierarchy - Network architecture: VPC design, subnets, firewall rules, interconnect - Compute architecture: service selection, container strategy, scaling model - Data architecture: database selection, BigQuery datasets, caching layer - Event architecture: Pub/Sub topology, Eventarc triggers, dead-letter handling - Security architecture: IAM hierarchy, service accounts, encryption, VPC controls - Observability architecture: monitoring workspace, log routing, trace sampling - IaC architecture: Terraform module structure, state backend, workspace strategy
artifact_overrides.risk_entry: - GCP service outages in specific regions - Cost overruns from BigQuery queries or unoptimized Cloud Run scaling - IAM misconfiguration granting excessive permissions - Pub/Sub message loss from unhandled errors and dead-letter overflow - Terraform state corruption or drift from manual console changes - Vendor lock-in from GCP-specific services (Spanner, Firestore) - GCP API deprecation requiring migration effort
